name: BaseInteractor
summary: base class for a PythonPart interactor

constructors.name: BaseInteractor
constructors.signature: BaseInteractor( coord_input: CoordinateInput, pyp_path: str, global_str_table_service: StringTableService, build_ele_list: list[BuildingElement], build_ele_composite: BuildingElementComposite, control_props_list: list[BuildingElementControlProperties], modify_uuid_list: list[str], )

params.name: global_str_table_service
params.doc: global string table service for default strings

params.name: build_ele_list
params.doc: list with the building elements containing parameter properties

params.name: modify_uuid_list
params.doc: UUIDs of the existing elements in the modification mode

methods.name: execute_load_favorite
methods.summary: Handles the execute load favorite event. This event is triggered after pressing "Load favorite" button in the property palette and selecting the favorite file in the file dialog Implementing this method is necessary only, when the load/save/restore favorite buttons are shown in the property palette, i.e. when the tag ShowFavoriteButtons in the .pyp file is set to True
methods.remarks: Handles the execute load favorite event. This event is triggered after pressing "Load favorite" button in the property palette and selecting the favorite file in the file dialog Implementing this method is necessary only, when the load/save/restore favorite buttons are shown in the property palette, i.e. when the tag ShowFavoriteButtons in the .pyp file is set to True

params.name: _file_name
params.doc: full path and name of the selected favorite file

methods.name: execute_save_favorite
methods.summary: Handles the execute save favorite event. This event is triggered after pressing "Save as a favorite" button in the property palette and selecting the location of the favorite file in the file dialog. Implementing this method is necessary only, when the load/save/restore favorite buttons are shown in the property palette, i.e. when the tag ShowFavoriteButtons in the .pyp file is set to True
methods.remarks: Handles the execute save favorite event. This event is triggered after pressing "Save as a favorite" button in the property palette and selecting the location of the favorite file in the file dialog. Implementing this method is necessary only, when the load/save/restore favorite buttons are shown in the property palette, i.e. when the tag ShowFavoriteButtons in the .pyp file is set to True

params.name: _file_name
params.doc: full path and name of the selected favorite file

methods.name: modify_element_property
methods.signature: modify_element_property(page: int, name: str, value: Any)
methods.summary: Handles the modify element property event. This event is triggered with each modification of the element property done in the property palette or by using a handle.
methods.remarks: Handles the modify element property event. This event is triggered with each modification of the element property done in the property palette or by using a handle.

params.name: value
params.doc: New value of the modified property.

methods.name: on_cancel_by_menu_function
methods.summary: Handles the event of terminating the PythonPart by calling another function in Allplan UI. Implement this method, when some actions must be introduced, before the PythonPart is eventually terminated.
methods.remarks: Handles the event of terminating the PythonPart by calling another function in Allplan UI. Implement this method, when some actions must be introduced, before the PythonPart is eventually terminated.

methods.name: on_cancel_function
methods.summary: Handles the cancel function event This event is triggered when the ESC button is hit during the runtime of the PythonPart.
methods.remarks: Handles the cancel function event This event is triggered when the ESC button is hit during the runtime of the PythonPart.

returns.doc: True when the PythonPart framework should terminate the PythonPart, False otherwise.

methods.name: on_control_event
methods.summary: Handles the on control event. Called when an event is triggered by a palette control (ex. button).
methods.remarks: Handles the on control event. Called when an event is triggered by a palette control (ex. button).

params.name: event_id
params.doc: event id of the clicked button control

methods.name: on_input_undo
methods.summary: Process the input undo event This event is triggered, when during coordinate input the user hits the undo button in the dialog line. Implementing this method is necessary only, when the undo button is shown to the user, i.e. the CoordinateInput.EnableUndoStep method was used.
methods.remarks: Process the input undo event This event is triggered, when during coordinate input the user hits the undo button in the dialog line. Implementing this method is necessary only, when the undo button is shown to the user, i.e. the CoordinateInput.EnableUndoStep method was used.

methods.name: on_mouse_leave
methods.summary: Handles the mouse leave event. This event is triggered, when the mouse leaves the viewport.
methods.remarks: Handles the mouse leave event. This event is triggered, when the mouse leaves the viewport.

methods.name: on_preview_draw
methods.summary: Handles the preview draw event. This event is triggered, when an input in the dialog line is done (e.g. input of a coordinate).
methods.remarks: Handles the preview draw event. This event is triggered, when an input in the dialog line is done (e.g. input of a coordinate).

methods.name: on_shortcut_control_input
methods.summary: Handles the input inside the shortcut control. This event is triggered, when a shortcut is hit inside a shortcut input control in the dialog line. A shortcut input control is a control in the dialog line, where the user can hit only certain keys, like e.g. ROTATION_ANGLE_STEP where only + and - keys can be hit. Implementing this method makes sense only, when this kind of input control is used!
methods.remarks: Handles the input inside the shortcut control. This event is triggered, when a shortcut is hit inside a shortcut input control in the dialog line. A shortcut input control is a control in the dialog line, where the user can hit only certain keys, like e.g. ROTATION_ANGLE_STEP where only + and - keys can be hit. Implementing this method makes sense only, when this kind of input control is used!

params.name: _value
params.doc: value associated with the hit shortcut key

methods.name: on_value_input_control_enter
methods.summary: Handles the value input control enter event. This event is triggered, when enter key is hit during the input inside the input control located in the dialog line.
methods.remarks: Handles the value input control enter event. This event is triggered, when enter key is hit during the input inside the input control located in the dialog line.

methods.name: process_mouse_msg
methods.signature: process_mouse_msg(mouse_msg: int, pnt: Point2D, msg_info: Any) -> bool
methods.summary: Handles the process mouse message event. This event is triggered with each message sent by the mouse, which can be a mouse move, mouse click, zoom out, etc. The message is sent only during mouse actions inside a viewport.
methods.remarks: Handles the process mouse message event. This event is triggered with each message sent by the mouse, which can be a mouse move, mouse click, zoom out, etc. The message is sent only during mouse actions inside a viewport.

params.name: pnt
params.doc: The input point in view coordinates. The origin is the mid point of the viewport

methods.name: reset_param_values
methods.summary: Handles the reset parameter values event. This event is triggered after pressing "Restore basic setting" button in the property palette. Implementing this method is necessary only, when the load/save/restore favorite buttons are shown in the property palette, i.e. when the tag ShowFavoriteButtons in the .pyp file is set to True
methods.remarks: Handles the reset parameter values event. This event is triggered after pressing "Restore basic setting" button in the property palette. Implementing this method is necessary only, when the load/save/restore favorite buttons are shown in the property palette, i.e. when the tag ShowFavoriteButtons in the .pyp file is set to True

methods.name: set_active_palette_page_index
methods.summary: Handles the event of changing pages inside the property palette
methods.remarks: Handles the event of changing pages inside the property palette

params.name: _active_page_index
params.doc: index of the active page, starting from 0

methods.name: start_next_input
methods.summary: start the next input Overload this member function to execute the needed steps after the execution of a script object interactor (e. g. after an element selection)
methods.remarks: start the next input Overload this member function to execute the needed steps after the execution of a script object interactor (e. g. after an element selection)

methods.name: update_after_favorite_read
methods.summary: Called after reading the favorite data Implementing this method is necessary only, when the execute_load_favorite method was implemented and additional actions must be performed after the favorite file has been loaded.
methods.remarks: Called after reading the favorite data Implementing this method is necessary only, when the execute_load_favorite method was implemented and additional actions must be performed after the favorite file has been loaded.
